import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut cur = 0
  mut sign = 1
  mut has = false
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(ch)
      if ch == 32 or ch == 9 or ch == 10 or ch == 13
        if has
          result = list.push(result, cur * sign)
          cur = 0
          sign = 1
          has = false
        end
      else if ch == 45
        sign = -1
      else if ch == 43
        sign = 1
      else
        cur = cur * 10 + (ch - 48)
        has = true
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    result = list.push(result, cur * sign)
  end
  result
end

fn sort_asc(_ xs: List<Int>) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut remaining = xs
  while remaining.len() > 0
    match list.min(remaining)
    when Some(m)
      result = list.push(result, m)
      mut new_rem: List<Int> = []
      mut removed = false
      for y in remaining
        if y == m and not removed
          removed = true
        else
          new_rem = list.push(new_rem, y)
        end
      end
      remaining = new_rem
    when None
      ()
    end
  end
  result
end

let line1 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let line2 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let a = parse_ints(line1)
let b = parse_ints(line2)

mut inter: List<Int> = []
for x in a
  if list.contains(b, x) and not list.contains(inter, x)
    inter = list.push(inter, x)
  end
end

let sorted = sort_asc(inter)

if sorted.len() == 0
  println("empty")
else
  mut out = ""
  mut first = true
  for x in sorted
    if first
      out = "#{x}"
      first = false
    else
      out = "#{out} #{x}"
    end
  end
  println(out)
end
